context_summary: Phase 19.4 SHIPPED at PASS. fraud-team K=10k zipfian filtered-median 102,800 EPS clears the v0 100k ship gate. Phase 19 verdict amended PASS-WITH-DEFICIT → PASS via cumulative 19.1/19.2/19.3/19.4 path. All 10 pipeline configs now have post-19.4 throughput baselines committed; 0 WARN, 0 BLOCK regressions. Apply-thread vertical optimization era is closed. Next phase per v0 critical path: Phase 14 (streaming correctness — fixes silent data-loss bug in agg_windowed bucket-reset). Plans 14-01..04 + 14-CONTEXT.md already written 2026-04-24; just need executor.

completed_this_session.result: WindowedOp::update_at architectural fix landed; +4.4% EPS / -1,526 ns agg-stage (within noise band). Predicted -3,900 ns lift was 60% overestimated due to cost-model conjecture in 19.2-INVESTIGATION.md §4. Plans 19.3-03/04/05 SUPERSEDED by Phase 19.4.
Predictions must come from flamegraph + observed counters, not per_call_ns × call_count arithmetic; subtract optimization's own overhead.
Dispatch-protocol refactors must enumerate ALL entry points incl. nested wrappers (WindowedOp etc.); use grep-count acceptance criteria.

decisions.decision: Phase 19.4 verdict accepted as PASS despite Plan 04 trace floor miss
decisions.rationale: Phase goal is 100k EPS; measured 102,800 EPS clears it. Plan 04's trace agg-stage drop was -100 ns vs predicted -1,200 ns, but the predicted scaffolding cost was outdated post-Plan-02 SmallVec cap-widening. All 4 correctness gates passed (bit-identical state, 1× rebuild count, 0 vec![] sites, clippy/fmt clean). Reverting would discard +3,800 EPS of real lift.

decisions.decision: Phase 19.5 sharding deployment moved OUT of v0 critical path
decisions.rationale: Phase 19.4 closes the apply-path 100k EPS gate; v0 ship is no longer perf-blocked. Sharding becomes nice-to-have for scale beyond 1 instance — moves to v0.1+ if customer demand. v0 critical path is correctness (Phase 14) + features (Phase 12 joins / 12.5 push_and_get / 16 SDK surface) + ship (Phase 13 follow-up).

decisions.decision: All 10 pipeline configs now have post-19.4 throughput baselines
decisions.rationale: Plan 05 only covered 5. User-requested follow-up benched the missing 5 at quieter load. Full bench coverage = clean regression-gate for Phase 19.5+ comparison.

decisions.decision: Cost model methodology change: flamegraph-derived predictions only
decisions.rationale: Memory feedback_cost_model_from_flamegraph saved. Phase 19.3-A's predicted -3,900 ns lift was 60% over (per_call_ns × call_count arithmetic ignored optimization's own overhead). Phase 19.4-A's predicted -1,180 ns lift was ~73% realized (sub-step attribution error). Phase 19.4-D's predicted -1,200 ns lift was ~8% realized (cost model drifted post-Plan-02). All future perf phases must cite flamegraph file:line in predicted_lift_source.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: Cost-model conjecture from per-call ns × call-count arithmetic
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Phase 19.3-A and 19.4-A both shipped architectural fixes correctly but missed predicted lift floors by 60-92% because the cost model was derived from `per_call_ns × call_count_per_event` arithmetic on microbench numbers. Three errors compound: (1) microbench per-call ns underestimates real cache/branch effects, (2) call-count ignores cluster amortization, (3) optimization's own overhead (new method body, additional indirection) is not subtracted.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: Memory feedback_cost_model_from_flamegraph saved. Every perf phase's predicted_lift field MUST cite a flamegraph file:line. Plan-checker rejects plans where predicted_lift_source is pure arithmetic without a flamegraph reference. Verified working: Phase 19.4 plans all cited 19.3-FLAMEGRAPH.md.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: Sub-step attribution error in flamegraph reading
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Phase 19.4-A attributed the full hashbrown::HashMap::insert self-time (1,234 ns/event = 9.36% apply CPU) to SipHash and predicted -1,180 ns lift from identity hasher swap. Actual lift was -866 ns (~73%). The remaining ~25% came from the SIMD probe-load, slot-fill check, eviction-tombstone scan, outer Hash::hash dispatch, and PartialEq compare which all survive the hasher swap. Only the inner <u64 as Hash>::hash body (~10-25 ns/call) was actually replaced.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: When predicting lift from a flamegraph hot function, decompose self-time into sub-steps; map each sub-step to whether the optimization actually replaces it. For HashMap::insert specifically: hasher swap only replaces the Hash::hash inner call, not the whole insert path.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: Cost-model drift across sequential plans
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Phase 19.4-D (ExtractedFields hoist) predicted -1,200 ns lift based on cost-model assumption '5×500 ns per-desc rebuild = 2,500 ns scaffolding'. Actual measured lift was -100 ns because Plan 19.4-02 (SmallVec cap-widening) had ALREADY eliminated the spill cost that was the bulk of per-desc rebuild. By the time Plan 04 ran, per-desc rebuild was ~50 ns × 5 = 250 ns total, not 2,500. Same flamegraph-attribution methodology, but the underlying source state had drifted between plans within the phase.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: For multi-plan phases with sequential gating: re-baseline the cost model AFTER each plan lands, not just at phase start. Future Phase 19.5+ flamegraph-derived plans should include a 'cost model re-baseline' task between sub-goal landings.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: System-load contamination of perf measurement
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Phase 19.4-02 measurement gate HALTed twice (load 6.67 attempt 1, load 9.4 attempt 2) before passing on attempt 3 (load ~5.6). Background processes (Playwright Chromium, Arc browser, Cursor IDE, Antigravity) consumed enough CPU to dominate fraud-team's measurement signal — EPS spread 41,680 across 22 runs at high load vs 2,741 across 3 runs at low load (15× wider noise band).
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: Quiet-system measurement protocol established: kill non-essential background processes before perf measurement; capture uptime BEFORE EACH RUN; use load-filter methodology (drop highest-2-of-7 runs, take median of 5). Documented in throughput-baselines.md `## 1M-event blast (rebaseline 19.4)` section caveat.

context_notes: The big architecture wins this session: (1) Phase 19.4 cleared the v0 100k EPS gate at 102.8k on fraud-team via 4 flamegraph-derived levers (CountDistinct identity hasher, SmallVec cap, geo lat_idx population, ExtractedFields hoist) — vertical optimization is essentially done for v0; (2) cost-model methodology pivoted from arithmetic-conjecture to flamegraph-derived (memory feedback_cost_model_from_flamegraph captures the lesson); (3) all 10 pipeline configs now have post-19.4 throughput baselines for clean Phase 19.5+ regression gating. The architectural pivot for v0 ship is now correctness + features + API stability, NOT more perf. Phase 14 → 15 → 14.1 → 12-followup → 12.5 → 16 → 13-followup → ship is the queue. Phase 19.5 sharding deployment is OUT of v0 critical path (only relevant if a customer needs >100k single-instance, which is uncommon for fraud-shape workloads where 1 instance handles a major issuer's TOTAL volume). Per memory `project_no_sharded_apply` the multi-instance Redis-cluster pattern is the answer for >100k EPS — this should be documented in Phase 19.5 deployment story BEFORE v0 ship if customers will encounter it.
